Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are given a temporal profile model and asked to identify anomalies generate hypotheses and propose SQL queries for verification We are to present our findings independently without referencing the instructions

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Step 1 Identify anomalies in the temporal profile model Step 2 Generate hypotheses for why these anomalies exist Step 3 Propose SQL queries to verify these hypotheses

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Lets go through each anomaly in the temporal profile

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 1 R P 90000 3600 Average time from Receive to Approve is 25 hours 90000 seconds with a standard deviation of 1 hour 3600 seconds The standard deviation is very low compared to the average which suggests that the time between these two activities is very consistent possibly too consistent for a manual process

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 2 P N 604800 172800 Average time from Approve to Notify is 7 days 604800 seconds with a standard deviation of 2 days 172800 seconds This is a long delay with a high standard deviation which might indicate that sometimes the notification is delayed a lot and sometimes its quicker

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 3 A C 7200 3600 Average time from Assign to Close is 2 hours 7200 seconds with a standard deviation of 1 hour 3600 seconds This is a relatively short time which might suggest that some claims are closed very soon after assignment possibly without going through all the necessary steps

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 4 E N 300 60 Average time from Evaluate to Notify is 5 minutes 300 seconds with a standard deviation of 1 minute 60 seconds This is a very short time which might indicate that the notification is happening almost immediately after evaluation possibly skipping the approval step or other steps

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Other pairs in the temporal profile seem more in line with expected process times

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: For each anomaly

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: 1 For R P Hypothesis The approval might be automated or happening in a batch process leading to a very consistent time Alternatively there might be a strict SLA that is being met by an automated system

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: 2 For P N Hypothesis The notification step might be manual and subject to varying workloads or backlogs It could also be that the notification is sent via a process that runs only at certain times causing variable delays

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: 3 For A C Hypothesis Some claims might be closed immediately after assignment because they are duplicates invalid or require no further action Alternatively the assignment might be to a resource that automatically closes claims without evaluation

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: 4 For E N Hypothesis The notification might be triggered automatically by the evaluation step or the evaluation and notification might be performed by the same person in quick succession This could also indicate that the approval step is skipped

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are to write queries on the claim_events table to investigate these anomalies We note that the claim_events table has columns event_id claim_id activity timestamp resource additional_info We are interested in the time differences between specific activities for each claim

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets write queries for each anomaly

Name: Validation - PE
Text: For R P We want to find claims where the time between Receive and Approve is very consistent low standard deviation and see if there are any patterns However the temporal profile is for the entire dataset so we might want to check the distribution of the time between R and P for claims that have both events

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We can compute the time difference for each claim and then look at the statistics but we are also interested in verifying if there are claims that have an unusually low time difference which might indicate automation Alternatively we can look at the time differences and see if they cluster around 25 hours

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query to get the time differences for each claim from R to P

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: But note the temporal profile already gives the average and standard deviation We are interested in the distribution and if there are clusters

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: We can also look at the distribution by time buckets Alternatively we can look for claims where the time is exactly 25 hours or within a small range and see if they share any common attributes

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query to find claims where the time between R and P is between 24 and 26 hours 86400 to 93600 seconds We can then check the claims and adjusters tables for these claims to see if there are any patterns

Name: Validation - PE
Text: For P N We want to find claims where the time between Approve and Notify is long say more than 5 days and see what might be causing the delay Query to find claims with P to N time greater than 5 days 432000 seconds We can then check if these claims are from a particular region adjuster or claim type

Name: Validation - PE
Text: For A C We want to find claims that are closed very quickly after assignment say within 3 hours and see if they are missing the Evaluate and Approve steps Query to find claims where A to C time is less than 3 hours 10800 seconds and check the events between A and C We can then look at the claim_events for these claims to see if they have the events in between

Name: Validation - PE
Text: For E N We want to find claims where the time between Evaluate and Notify is very short say less than 10 minutes and see if they are missing the Approve step Query to find claims where E to N time is less than 10 minutes 600 seconds Then for these claims we can check if they have an P Approve event between E and N

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: We can also check if the resource for E and N is the same which might indicate that the same person evaluated and notified quickly Alternatively we can check the entire sequence of events for these claims

Name: Validation - PE
Text: However note that the above queries are just starting points We can adjust the thresholds and also look at the distribution of these events by different dimensions adjuster region claim type etc
